channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Octavio Dotel, who once held record of pitching for 13 major league teams, dies in DR roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Octavio Dotel, who pitched for 13 major league teams in a 15-year career and won a World Series with the St. Louis Cardinals, was among the dead after a roof collapsed at a nightclub in his native Dominican Republic where he was attending a merengue concert.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Slumping Max Homa from Cal arrives at Masters having changed everything except his results
item.description: Six-time PGA Tour winner who rose to No. 5 in the world, Homa is now ranked 81st and hasn’t made a cut against a full field since last year’s British Open.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Baseball: Canyon Hills 10, Southwest 7
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Great Monday to play some baseball with Southwest hosting Canyon Hills. Rattlers go on to win a score fest 10-7. The Rattlers scored six in the first and held on to win, despite Southwest cutting it down to 9-7 before tacking on one more run to win by three.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Michael Malone takes the fall despite being the least of the Nuggets' multitude of headaches
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Jamal Murray’s delicate health. Michael Porter Jr.’s dizzying slumps. Aaron Gordon’s balky right calf. Russell Westbrook’s boneheaded plays. An indifferent defense. A paper-thin bench. An ever-changing starting lineup.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bend it like Declan: Rice curls home two free kicks as Arsenal beats Real Madrid 3-0 in CL quarters
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Declan Rice put Arsenal on the brink of reaching the Champions League semifinals by curling home two free kicks that Real Madrid’s most famous “galacticos” would have been proud of.
item.dc:creator: MATTIAS KAREN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kane misses, Frattesi scores as Inter beats Bayern 2-1 in 1st leg of Champions League quarterfinals
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Inter Milan’s treble chances are still very much on after it won 2-1 at Bayern Munich in the first leg of the Champions League quarterfinals Tuesday to inflict the German team's first home defeat in the competition in almost exactly four years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jon Rahm and Bryson DeChambeau are back in the fold at the Masters. It's a rare occasion
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — For now, there's another tradition unlike any other at the Masters: The first opportunity in nine months for all the world's best players to compete against each other.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sharks prospect talks flying jitters, Smith & Celebrini, his trade request
item.description: San Jose Sharks prospect Quentin Musty, a 2023 first-round pick, could make pro debut Friday for San Jose Barracuda
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Ex-Northwestern players settle alleged hazing lawsuit against school, former head coach Pat Fitzgerald: report
item.description: Ex-Northwestern football players have reportedly settled their alleged hazing lawsuit against the school and former head coach Pat Fitzgerald.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Agreement in works to settle Northwestern hazing lawsuits that led to football coach's firing
item.description: Former Northwestern University football players are finalizing an agreement with the school to settle lawsuits alleging hazing and abuse on the team that led to longtime coach Pat Fitzgerald’s firing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nuggets fire coach Malone and won't extend GM Booth in stunning move as postseason looms
item.description: Michael Malone coached the Denver Nuggets to the NBA title in 2023. He has them set to make the postseason for a seventh consecutive year. Even amid a four-game slide, they're still in position to have home-court advantage in Round 1.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former Detroit Tigers pitcher among at least 44 killed in Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: A former Detroit Tigers pitcher was among at least 44 people killed when the roof of a nightclub collapsed in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: Thousands of intimate images found in hacking investigation of former NFL and Michigan coach
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Investigators have seized thousands of intimate photos and videos from a former Baltimore Ravens and University of Michigan assistant football coach who is charged with hacking into the computer accounts of college athletes, the U.S. Justice Department told victims.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nuggets fire head coach, GM with three games remaining before the playoffs
item.description: The decision comes as the Nuggets only have three regular-season games remaining before the NBA playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: Maine lawmakers send letter to Trump condemning Gov Janet Mills over trans athletes in girls' sports policies
item.description: A letter signed by 14 Maine state senators condemning Gov. Janet Mills was sent to the White House amid an ongoing battle over trans athlete policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump administration visa policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, WGN 9

item.title: Astros' Spencer Arrighetti breaks thumb in freak batting practice accident
item.description: Houston Astros starting pitcher Spencer Arrighetti suffered an unfortunate injury in a freak batting practice accident on Monday prior to the team's game against the Mariners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Olivia Dunne testifies against $2.8B NCAA settlement
item.description: LSU gymnast Olivia Dunne expressed why she is against the $2.8 billion NCAA settlement that would allow college athletes to get paid by their schools.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Retzlaff poised for another big year at BYU
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Last season, BYU quarterback Jake Retzlaff threw for almost 3,000 yards, 20 touchdowns, while rushing for over 400 yards and six TD's as the Cougars shocked everyone with an 11-2 season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Northwestern University to settle lawsuit over football program hazing allegations
item.description: Northwestern University has agreed to settle a lawsuit filed by students against the school and former head coach Pat Fitzgerald, who alleged hazing incidents took place in the football program, but the settlement does not resolve Fitzgerald's lawsuit against the university.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: MLB batting average drops again to .239, near record low in 1968's Year of the Pitcher
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Offense is down again at the start of the big league season, with hits even harder to come by. The big league batting average was .239 through the first full week of play, down from .240 through the first full week of 2024.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida makes push all the way to NCAA title game, will face Houston team that pushes others around
item.description: Florida has gone from being picked to finish sixth in the rugged Southeastern Conference to pushing all the way to the final Monday night of the season.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jaquan Brisker's bond with Bears' DC Dennis Allen predates his NFL career
item.description: Jaquan Brisker was originally drafted by the Saints, but instead joined the Bears, where he will be reunited with his former defensive coordinator, Dennis Allen, who is now the Bears' defensive coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: LA Dodgers visit White House to celebrate World Series win
item.description: Team president Stan Kasten said all the players wanted to attend, according to the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: UConn dominates South Carolina in women's national championship
item.description: A huge win for the UConn Huskies Sunday as the team dominated the South Carolina Gamecocks and made history inside Amalie Arena.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coby White scores 37, Josh Giddey adds 23 as Bulls beat Hornets 131-117 for 3rd straight win
item.description: Coby White scored 37 points, shooting 6 of 8 from 3-point range, Josh Giddey added 23 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists and the Chicago Bulls rolled past the Charlotte Hornets 131-117 on Sunday for their third straight win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Torkelson's 2-run double in the ninth inning gives Tigers 4-3 win over White Sox
item.description: Spencer Torkelson smacked a two-run double to cap Detroit's three-run rally in the ninth inning and give the Tigers a 4-3 win over the Chicago White Sox on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UConn returns to top of women's basketball, dominates South Carolina 82-59 to capture its 12th title
item.description: Dawn Staley and South Carolina hope to finish off a second straight season with an NCAA championship, which would give the Gamecocks a third title in four years. To do so, they'll need to beat Paige Bueckers and UConn on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida makes push all the way to NCAA title game, set to face Houston
item.description: Florida has gone from being picked to finish sixth in the rugged Southeastern Conference to pushing all the way to the final Monday night of the season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cleveland's José Ramírez hits 3 homers and claims another piece of Guardians history
item.description: José Ramírez hit three homers and carried the Guardians to a victory Friday night. The slugger claimed another place in Cleveland baseball history in the process.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Defending national champion South Carolina returns to women's title game with 74-57 rout of Texas
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Dawn Staley has never lost a national championship game in three trips as South Carolina's coach. Part of the key to winning those decisive games? Forgetting about what happened the last time she was there. And this time, that was only a year ago.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Imanaga and Happ lead the Cubs past the Padres 3-1 in Wrigley Field opener
item.description: Shota Imanaga pitched one-run ball into the eighth inning in Chicago's home opener at Wrigley Field, and the Cubs handed San Diego its first loss with a 3-1 victory over the Padres on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Two De Beque Dragons sign to college: Ava Vines and Allyson Sandidge
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Despite having a population of less than 500, despite having just a small 1A school, De Beque, Colorado, and the De Beque Dragons have had a lineage of college athletes come through their program.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Chicagoland foursome heads to Augusta for Drive, Chip and Putt Finals
item.description: GOLF, Ill. - Mastering golf can take a lifetime. Luckily, these four started early. "When I was like 2, I was holding a plastic club, watching a golf tournament - I don't remember which one," remembered 12-year-old Winnetka native Jack Kemper.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: Trevor Lewis excited to be back in Utah for NHL game
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah native Trevor Lewis has played a few preseason games in Salt Lake City, but never a regular season game. So as he returns to his home state to play against the Utah Hockey Club with the Los Angeles Kings tonight, he couldn't be more excited.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utes ready to rock the Regionals
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah gymnastics team is the only program in the nation that has qualified for all 48 NCAA National Championship gymnastics meets.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Teaching Chicago's hard-working values, basketball legend Tom Kleinschmidt builds a dynasty at DePaul Prep
item.description: DePaul College Prep's boys' basketball team, coached by Tom Kleinschmidt, has won three consecutive IHSA championships, thanks to his hard work, discipline, and respect, which he learned growing up on the city's Northwest Side.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9
